name: GLM-5.2 (High)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, 744B total / 40B active, DSA + 'IndexShare' sparse-attention indexer reuse (2.9x fewer per-token FLOPs at 1M context), 1M-token context; selectable High/Max reasoning-effort modes
note: Default reasoning-effort tier: 'High' mode balances strong performance against latency/token cost for everyday coding, per Z.ai's own guidance. Same weights as the Max tier, selected via the reasoning_effort API parameter.
verificationNotes:
GLM-5.2 confirmed real and released via Hugging Face model card + blog (zai-org/GLM-5.2, huggingface.co/blog/zai-org/glm-52-blog), zai-org/GLM-5 GitHub repo, official docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5.2 (1M context, 128K max output, benchmark claims), and heavy independent coverage (VentureBeat, MarkTechPost, Pandaily, DataCamp, Together AI, Eigent.ai) of the June 2026 launch. Release date has conflicting reports: eigent.ai and Pandaily say June 13, 2026; docs.z.ai's own release-notes timeline and llm-stats.com say June 16/17 -- used June 13 as the more specific, earliest-cited date, but this is a genuine multi-day uncertainty. This entry represents the 'High' reasoning-effort tier. Z.ai's own MarkTechPost-covered launch explicitly did NOT publish a full official per-tier benchmark breakdown ('No Benchmarks at Launch' per that outlet's headline); the self-reported numbers that later appeared on the HF blog and in docs.z.ai are believed to represent the top (Max) configuration, so they are attached to the GLM-5.2 (Max) sibling entry rather than this one, and this entry's results are left empty to avoid fabricating a High-tier number that was never actually published. Sources also disagree on which tier is the true API default: eigent.ai's blog explicitly states 'High is the default mode ... Max raises the reasoning budget' (used here), but one WebSearch synthesis of another source claimed the opposite ('Max is the default and High is opt-in') and docs.z.ai's own code example shows reasoning_effort='max' in a sample call. Flagged as uncertain -- see 'uncertain' field. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): Final diversified search for GLM-5.2 (High) (corpus/models/zhipu-ai-z-ai-tsinghua/glm-5-2-high.json) found ZERO citable benchmark results specific to the "High" reasoning-effort tier, confirming the entry should remain empty. Read existing verificationNotes first (already documented Z.ai's launch had no benchmark table, and later-published numbers were attributed to the Max sibling entry).

Searched this round: Chinese-language sources (智谱/GLM-5.2 跑分 via 36Kr, DataLearnerAI, Zhihu (知乎), 量子位/QbitAI, docs.bigmodel.cn official Chinese docs), OpenCompass/司南 (no GLM-5.2 entry found), SuperCLUE (no GLM-5.2 entry found), Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/models/glm-5-2 — page and Intelligence Index score of 51 / GDPval-AA 1524 Elo are explicitly labeled "GLM-5.2 (max)"), GitHub README (zai-org/GLM-5, fetched raw), Hugging Face blog, OpenRouter, Design Arena/LMArena-style leaderboards, llm-stats.com, BenchLM.ai.

Decisive finding: the official zai-org/GLM-5 GitHub README states verbatim: "`max` is the default... For default scenarios such as benchmark/leaderboard reproduction, keep `Max` (no setting required); only set `reasoning_effort=\"high\"` when you specifically want the `High` level." This confirms every published GLM-5.2 benchmark number (HLE, GPQA Diamond, AIME 2026, SWE-bench Pro, Terminal-Bench 2.1, GDPval-AA, AA Intelligence Index, etc., appearing on the official model card, Artificial Analysis, HF blog, and DataLearnerAI) reflects the Max configuration, not High — this is a structural reason (Z.ai doesn't benchmark High), not merely an absence of coverage. No vendor Chinese-language post, Chinese leaderboard, or independent outlet publishes a High-tier-specific number. z.ai/blog/glm-5.2 itself returned empty/JS-rendered content on fetch and could not be independently re-verified, but all secondary sources describing its table content treat it as a single unified (Max) score set.

Conclusion: no additions. This is the expected, honest outcome for a reasoning-effort variant whose vendor explicitly recommends and uses only the sibling (Max) tier for all public benchmark reporting. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): Checked the two specific structured sources requested for GLM-5.2 (High) (corpus/models/zhipu-ai-z-ai-tsinghua/glm-5-2-high.json) — no High-tier-specific results found in either, consistent with and reinforcing all prior rounds' finding that Z.ai only benchmarks/publishes the Max reasoning-effort configuration.

1. Epoch AI (https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json and https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv, both fetched directly): benchmarks.json is benchmark-definition metadata only (no model scores). benchmarks.csv is the real results feed (999 rows) and contains exactly one GLM-5.2 model id: "glm-5.2_max" (8 rows: EBR-bench 0.095, SWE-Bench Verified 0.787, OTIS Mock AIME 2024-2025 0.864, GPQA Diamond 0.919, FrontierMath-Tiers-1-3-v2-Private 0.592, FrontierMath-Tier-4-v2-Private 0.293, SimpleQA Verified 0.381, Chess Puzzles 0.21; all dated 2026-06-16). No "glm-5.2_high" or unsuffixed "glm-... [truncated; see round13_result.json for full text]

[ROUND 20 — HF-raw-content + academic-citation search, 2026-07-16] GLM-5.2 (High) — corpus/models/zhipu-ai-z-ai-tsinghua/glm-5-2-high.json — ROUND-14, HF-raw-content + Semantic-Scholar-citation check (2026-07-16). Result: zero new additions; entry correctly remains empty.

1) HuggingFace model card RAW content — HAS an HF repo (zai-org/GLM-5.2), checked directly, not via search-engine summary. Fetched https://huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-5.2/raw/main/README.md in full (106 lines). The single benchmark table (lines 42-65) has one 'GLM-5.2' column — no High/Max split anywhere in the card. Also queried https://huggingface.co/api/models/zai-org/GLM-5.2 for 'Files and versions': found an .eval_results/ directory (tag 'eval-results') containing 4 YAML files — deep-swe.yaml, gpqa.yaml, hle.yaml, swe-bench_pro.yaml — fetched all 4 raw; each just restates the same README numbers (HLE 40.5 / 54.7-with-tools, GPQA-Diamond 91.2, deep-swe 46.2, SWE-bench Pro 62.1) with a 'source: Model Card' pointer and no effort-tier field. No eval_results.json existed (only the 4 yamls above); no other model-card variant/mirror repo was found.

New decisive evidence (not previously quoted in the corpus's verificationNotes) that ALL these HF-hosted numbers are the Max tier, not High: the model's chat_template_jinja (returned in the HF API's config metadata) hard-codes: "{%- set effective_reasoning_effort = 'high' if reasoning_effort is defined and reasoning_effort == 'high' else 'max' -%}" — i.e. Max is the template's own fallback/default. Additionally the README's own evaluation footnotes state verbatim: for ProgramBench, "...reasoning_effort=max..."; and for FrontierSWE / PostTrainBench / SWE-Marathon, "The evaluation was conducted by [X] with 1M context length, max effort level, and 128K maximum output tokens." This is a first-party, quotable, machine-readable confirmation (template default + eval footnotes) reinforcing rounds 7 and 13's finding via the GitHub README, now sourced from the model card/repo itself.

2) Semantic Scholar + Google Scholar citation search for third-party papers reporting GLM-5.2 comparison-table scores — attempted directly against api.semanticscholar.org. The single-paper search endpoint (/graph/v1/paper/search?query=GLM-5.2) returned HTTP 429 'Too Many Requests' on 6 consecutive attempts across several minutes of backoff (no API key available in this environment). The /graph/v1/paper/search/bulk endpoint did succeed and returned 122 hits, but essentially all are false positives on 'GLM' as the statistics term 'Generalized Linear Model' (unrelated to the LLM). Checked the handful of plausibly LLM-related titles by name via WebSearch/arXiv: 'The Rise of Verbal Tics in Large Language Models' (arXiv:2604.19139) — 8-model comparison (GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.7, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Grok 4.2, Doubao-Seed-2.0-pro, Kimi K2.5, DeepSeek V3.2, MiMo-V2-Pro) does NOT include GLM-5.2 at all. 'Peer-Preservation in Frontier Models' (arXiv:2604.19784) — uses GLM 4.7, not GLM-5.2. 'Memory Scarcity, Open Models, and the Restructuring of the AI Industry, 2026-2030' (arXiv:2607.07207, submitted 2026-07-08) — mentions GLM-5.2 only as industry-economics context ("frontier-capable open-weight models exemplified by GLM-5.2"); directly WebFetched the arXiv abstract page and confirmed no benchmark table or GLM-5.2 score of any kind, let alone High-tier. GLM-5.2 itself has no dedicated arXiv paper (the only zai-org arXiv paper found, 2602.15763 'GLM-5: from Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering', predates the 5.2 release and cannot contain 5.2 scores). No comparison-table citation of GLM-5.2 by any third-party academic paper was found — consistent with the model being ~1 month old (released 2026-06-13) at time of search, likely too recent for peer-reviewed citing papers to exist yet.

Conclusion: both requested source types were checked directly (not just re-summarized from prior rounds). Neither surfaced a High-tier-specific number. The HF chat-template + eval-footnote evidence is new and strengthens (does not merely repeat) the existing structural explanation already in verificationNotes: Z.ai does not benchmark the High tier at all, only Max, so no honest High-tier addition is possible without fabrication. No additions made.

[ROUND 21 — light delta-check, 2026-07-16] GLM-5.2 (High) — corpus/models/zhipu-ai-z-ai-tsinghua/glm-5-2-high.json. Per instructions, since the prior round (Round 20) was dated the same day (2026-07-16), this was a light re-check, not a full re-derivation. WebSearched for High-tier-specific benchmark numbers (GPQA/HLE/SWE-bench-Pro/AIME); every hit (BenchLM.ai, apidog.com, technology.org, bitsminds.com, codersera.com, emergent.sh) reproduces the same GPQA-Diamond 91.2 / HLE 54.7-with-tools / 40.5-no-tools / SWE-bench Pro 62.1 figures already attributed to the Max sibling entry, with no High-specific breakout anywhere. Re-fetched z.ai/blog/glm-5.2 directly: still returns empty/JS-rendered content, unchanged from prior rounds. One new but low-confidence data point surfaced: a third-party community "abliterated" quantization repo (huggingface.co/zandenAI/GLM-5.2-FP8-Uncensored) states its own eval methodology as "thinking-ON, reasoning_effort=high, temperature 1.0, top_p 0.95" and reports a self-run of GPQA-Diamond 91.0 / HLE 40.8 / AIME-2026 99.2 -- nearly identical to Z.ai's official Max-attributed figures (91.2 / 40.5 / 99.2) -- while explicitly stating "I did not re-run benchmarks on the stock model; this table compares my abliterated model's results against Z.ai's published figures." This is a single non-expert, non-Z.ai community source and conflicts with the stronger first-party evidence already on record (the official zai-org/GLM-5 GitHub README's explicit "max is default... for benchmark/leaderboard reproduction, keep Max" statement, the model's own chat_template_jinja default fallback to 'max', and the HF eval-result YAML footnotes citing "max effort level"). Treated as not credible enough to reattribute Z.ai's published numbers to the High tier or to add any High-tier-specific result. Conclusion unchanged: no additions made, entry correctly remains empty.
